data.title: Microservices Structure
data.description: Apple's website architecture utilizes a microservices framework to enable modular development and rapid updates.
data.technical_spec: Decomposed services connected through REST APIs with deployment using container orchestration platforms like Kubernetes.

data.title: Responsive Design
data.description: The site employs responsive design to ensure seamless performance across devices.
data.technical_spec: Implemented with CSS media queries and flexible grids, ensuring UI consistency from desktop to mobile.

data.title: Content Delivery Network
data.description: Global content distribution to enhance page load speed and performance.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes advanced CDN services like Akamai to cache and deliver content closer to users.

data.title: Progressive Enhancement
data.description: Progressive enhancement strategy ensures essential content is accessible regardless of browser capability.
data.technical_spec: Implementation focuses on delivering core features first, layered with advanced functionalities.

data.title: Scalable Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Dynamic scaling of computational resources to handle variable loads.
data.technical_spec: Built on cloud platforms enabling elasticity, such as AWS or Apple-specific solutions.
